item.title: Teva Austedo
item.description:
• Summary: Austedo (deutetrabenazine) has been a key driver of Teva's financial turnaround, with strong sales signaling the company's successful metamorphosis. The VMAT2 inhibitor is approved for chorea associated with Huntington's disease and tardive dyskinesia, and its market performance underscores Teva's strategic shift toward innovative therapies.
  • MoAs: Austedo (deutetrabenazine): Vesicular monoamine transporter 2 (VMAT2) inhibitor
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma

item.title: Newron Schizophrenia Trial Clinical Hold
item.description:
• Summary: The FDA placed a clinical hold on Newron's schizophrenia trial after a patient death. The trial was evaluating evenamide, a sodium channel blocker designed to modulate glutamate release. The hold raises questions about the safety profile of the investigational drug and its future development.
  • MoAs: Evenamide: Use-dependent sodium channel blocker that inhibits glutamate release
  • Sources: Fierce Biotech

item.title: Biogen Alzheimer's Transition from Kisunla
item.description:
• Summary: Biogen is positioning to capture Alzheimer's patients transitioning off Lilly's Kisunla (donanemab), as the drug's limited duration regimen opens opportunity for Leqembi (lecanemab).
  • MoAs: Anti-amyloid beta monoclonal antibodies
  • Sources: Biospace

item.title: Novartis PTC Huntington's Drug
item.description:
• Summary: Novartis and PTC Therapeutics are advancing a Huntington's disease drug into Phase 3 trials, but remain tight-lipped about pursuing accelerated approval. The therapy targets the underlying genetic cause of Huntington's by modulating splicing to reduce production of the toxic huntingtin protein. Progress in this program is closely watched given the high unmet need in neurodegenerative disorders.
  • MoAs: Splicing modulator targeting huntingtin protein
  • Sources: BioSpace
